title: LCGD Conjecture
statement_text: For every graph $G$, the genus polynomial $\Gamma_G(x) = \sum_{k \geq 0} g_k(G)x^k$ is log-concave.
context_text: The Log-Concavity Genus Distribution (LCGD) Conjecture was proposed by Gross, Robbins, and Tucker in 1989. It asserts that the sequence of coefficients $g_0(G), g_1(G), \ldots$ satisfies $(g_k(G))^2 \geq g_{k-1}(G)g_{k+1}(G)$ for every $k$. The conjecture has been confirmed for various graph classes but remains widely open for general graphs.
paper_contribution: Identifies six cubic graphs (including generalized Petersen graphs $G(8,2)$, $G(10,2)$, $G(12,2)$, and three others) whose genus polynomials are log-concave overall yet possess quadratic real factors that are not log-concave, clarifying the boundary between real-rootedness and log-concavity in relation to this conjecture.
paper_title: Genus Polynomials of Cubic Graphs with Non-Real Roots

title: Conjecture 1 (Stahl)
statement_text: For every graph $G$, the genus polynomial $\Gamma_G(x)$ has only real roots.
context_text: Stahl conjectured in 1997 that genus polynomials of all graphs are real-rooted, a property that would have implied the LCGD Conjecture via log-concavity preservation under multiplication. Chen and Liu disproved this conjecture in 2010 by exhibiting two graphs with non-real roots; the present paper significantly extends the set of known counterexamples.
paper_contribution: By computing genus polynomials for all cubic graphs with up to 16 vertices, identifies 226 cubic graphs whose genus polynomials have at least one non-real root, and exhibits six specific graphs whose complex roots satisfy $|\Re(z)| < |\Im(z)|/\sqrt{3}$, lying outside the cone guaranteed to yield log-concave quadratic factors.
paper_title: Genus Polynomials of Cubic Graphs with Non-Real Roots
